originalTitle: Fifa plays down reports that ticketless fans breached security at England World Cup game
summary: Fifa have played down reports that ticketless England supporters were able to gain entry for the World Cup opener against Croatia after evading security checks at the Dallas Stadium
content: Fifa has played down reports that ticketless England supporters were able to gain entry for the World Cup opener against Croatia after evading security checks at the Dallas Stadium. An unspecified number of fans without tickets are said to have made their way into the ground despite a huge security operation being put in place at the home of the Dallas Cowboys in Arlington. Officials said that snipers were in place inside the stadium, with the Arlington police department deploying “highly trained personnel and specialised resources” at the venue. But despite those measures and some fans having paid thousands of pounds for tickets, there were widespread reports of supporters without tickets gaining access. “It was ridiculous,” one fan told the Daily Mail. “There were big gaps at the side of the ticket barriers and people just strolled through. There were volunteers who were basically old ladies and they weren’t stopping anyone. I scanned my ticket because I had one, but nobody checked my flag or my trophy. Loads just walked through while others just jumped over the top. There were huge gaps.” Another told the Times: “The security checks weren’t great. They wanted people to flash tickets, but many didn’t. The turnstile itself was quite easy to jump over. Quite a few managed to jib in.” A Fifa spokesperson said: “At this stage, we have no indication of fans entering the stadium without a valid match ticket for the game in question.” Some England fans were also forced to remove flags from certain areas of the ground after stewards intervened. According to the Telegraph, they threatened to confiscate the flags if they weren’t taken down. One fan from London, Dan, who had an Arsenal and England flag, said: “For the amount of money we have paid it is a joke. But what can you do? It is another occasion where football fans are getting treated like cattle.”
chineseTitle: 国际足联淡化有关无票球迷在英格兰世界杯比赛中违反安全措施的报道
chineseSummary: 国际足联淡化了有关无票英格兰支持者在逃避达拉斯体育场安检后能够进入世界杯揭幕战对阵克罗地亚的报道
chineseContent: 国际足联淡化了有关无票英格兰支持者在逃避达拉斯体育场安检后能够进入世界杯揭幕战对阵克罗地亚的报道。尽管达拉斯牛仔队阿灵顿主场采取了大规模的安全措施，但据说仍有数量不详的没有门票的球迷进入球场。官员们表示，体育场内部署了狙击手，阿灵顿警察局在场馆部署了“训练有素的人员和专业资源”。尽管采取了这些措施，并且一些球迷支付了数千英镑购买门票，但仍有大量报道称支持者没有门票。 “这太荒谬了，”一位粉​​丝告诉《每日邮报》。 “检票栏旁边有很大的缝隙，人们只是漫步穿过。有志愿者，基本上都是老太太，她们没有阻止任何人。我扫描了我的票，因为我有一张，但没有人检查我的旗帜或我的奖杯。很多人只是走过，而其他人只是跳过顶部。有很大的缝隙。”另一位人士告诉《泰晤士报》：“安全检查不太好。他们希望人们出示门票，但很多人没有。旋转栅门本身很容易跳过。不少人设法钻了进去。”国际足联发言人表示：“现阶段，我们没有迹象表明球迷在没有相关比赛的有效比赛门票的情况下进入体育场。”在工作人员介入后，一些英格兰球迷还被迫移除球场某些区域的旗帜。据《每日电讯报》报道，他们威胁称，如果不拆除这些旗帜，他们将没收这些旗帜。 One fan from London, Dan, who had an Arsenal and England flag, said: “For the amount of money we have paid it is a joke. But what can you d
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
